step_by_step_thinking:
To compare the means of continuous measurements among four different groups (non-smokers, light smokers, moderate smokers, and heavy smokers), specifically focusing on the similarity of the mean peak flow rate, an appropriate statistical test would involve assessing variance across these groups. Since it involves analyzing the variation and differences in means among more than two independent groups, **One-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA)** is the most suitable approach here. Hence, the optimal statistical test for determining similarities/differences in mean peak flows due to varying smoking statuses is:
\boxed{A} Option A: One way ANOVA.
```markdown
# Explanation
Given that we are dealing with four distinct categories (smoking status groups) and measuring a continuous variable (mean peak flow rate), a one-way ANOVA is the right choice to evaluate whether there are any statistically significant differences in the means among these groups.
```
Therefore, my final answer is \(\boxed{A}\). ```markdown
